input: # Note: per EDTF grammar Feb 29 syntactically parses for any year;
# 1900-02-29 and 2019-02-29 are accepted by the parser as round-trip
# valid, although calling min() / max() on them will throw because
# java.time rejects the impossible date. We only check round-trip
# Unicode minus sign U+2212 in offset
# Y-notation: 5+ digits required after the Y prefix.
# Values up to ±290 million are representable as long-epoch-ms;
# beyond that, parsing succeeds but min() / max() throw
# ArithmeticException. See L1ParserTest for that overflow path.
# Empty set / list (no markers, no members)
